title: cytisus scoparius subsp. cantabricus
intro: Cytisus scoparius subsp. cantabricus, also known as Cantabricus broom, is a subspecies of the broom plant (Cytisus scoparius), a flowering plant native to the Cantabrian Mountains in Spain and Portugal. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cytisus scoparius subsp. cantabricus include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. This plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be consumed as teas, capsules, or added to topical creams and ointments.
